summary:
Completed the fix for integration routes silently swallowing persistence and partner failures via .ok() in quickbooks.rs. The file had already been partially fixed in a prior session (23 of 24 .ok() calls). This session:

1. Fixed the last remaining .ok() on get_company_info() (line 260) - replaced `.map_err(|e| { warn!(...); e }).ok()` with an explicit `match` that logs a warning and returns None (Category C: optional enrichment).

2. Fixed the review gap in update_account_mappings: added error counter that tracks failed mapping updates. Returns 500 if ALL updates fail, otherwise includes `errors` count in the response JSON instead of always returning `{"status": "updated"}`.

3. Added 2 new tests: test_update_account_mappings_response_includes_errors (partial failure) and test_update_account_mappings_response_zero_errors (success case).

Verification: grep confirms 0 .ok() calls remaining, 0 .unwrap() calls introduced. Both files pass syntax checks. Commit ad566231 on branch fix/qb-ok-silence.
decisions_made: update_account_mappings returns Internal (500) error when ALL mappings fail, but partial failures are reported via errors field in response
get_company_info failure is non-blocking (Category C) - logs warning and proceeds without company name
open_questions: xero.rs has identical .ok() pattern and needs the same fix as a follow-up PR
edi.rs has 3 instances, other integration files (bill_com.rs, salesforce.rs, etc.) have 20-23 instances each
next_steps: Apply identical .ok() removal pattern to xero.rs (follow-up PR)
Run SQLX_OFFLINE=true cargo build && cargo test to verify full compilation
Consider same fix for edi.rs, bill_com.rs, salesforce.rs, sage_intacct.rs, workday.rs
